detaileddescription.para: In this case, the text is exact and not left to the particular implementation; it must read: MARK yyyy = mmmm where yyyy is User-process data stream marker, and mmmm server's equivalent marker (note the spaces between markers and "=")

briefdescription.para: Data connection already open; transfer starting.
File status okay; about to open data connection.
Command not implemented, superfluous at this site.
System status, or system help reply.

detaileddescription.para: Explains how to use the server or the meaning of a particular non-standard command. This reply is useful only to the human user
Where NAME is an official system name from the registry kept by IANA.

briefdescription.para: Data connection open; no transfer in progress.

detaileddescription.para: Requested file action successful (for example, file transfer or file abort)

briefdescription.para: Entering Long Passive Mode (long address, port)
Logout command noted, will complete when transfer done.
Specifies that the server accepts the authentication mechanism specified by the client, and the exchange of security data is complete.

detaileddescription.para: A higher level nonstandard code created by Microsoft

briefdescription.para: Requested file action pending further information.
Service not available, closing control connection.

detaileddescription.para: This may be a reply to any command if the service knows it must shut down
Insufficient storage space in system. File unavailable (for example, file busy)

briefdescription.para: Syntax error in parameters or arguments.
Command not implemented for that parameter.
Could Not Connect to Server - Policy Requires SSL.

detaileddescription.para: File unavailable (for example, file not found, no access)
Exceeded storage allocation (for current directory or dataset)
The connection was forcibly closed by the remote host
The connection is actively refused by the server

briefdescription.para: Too many users, server is full.
List of FTP server reply codes Reference RFC959 FTP Transfer Protocol.
Write to a file with a unique file name.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* ftp_client_callback_t) (const uint8_t *msg, uint16_t len)
memberdef.name: ftp_client_callback_t

parameterdescription.para: FTP client data received, or local message

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct ftp_client *client, const char *hostname, uint16_t port, int sec_tag)
memberdef.name: ftp_open

parameterdescription.para: FTP server name or IP address
If FTP over TLS is required (-1 means no TLS)

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.

briefdescription.para: Get FTP server and connection status Also returns server system type.

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.

briefdescription.para: List information of folder or file.

parameterdescription.para: List options, refer to Linux "man ls"
file or directory to list. If not specified, list current folder

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct ftp_client *client, const char *file, const uint8_t *data, uint16_t length, int type)
memberdef.name: ftp_put

briefdescription.para: Put data to a file If file does not exist, create the file.

parameterdescription.para: Length of data to be stored
specify FTP put types, see enum ftp_reply_code

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.

briefdescription.para: Exchange keep-alive commands with the server.

simplesect.para: 0 on success. A negative errno code in case of a failure. A positive FTP status code in case of an unexpected server response.
